title: Class-aware dry versus wet next-stop feasibility
summary: Separate dry-slick strategy windows from wet/intermediate survival windows instead of treating all compounds as one tyre class.
rationale: The raw data contains slick, intermediate, and wet compounds, and several run variants attempted class-aware feasibility. Separate representation can prevent wet-weather rows from diluting dry-compound final-stop logic.
implementation_hint: Create compound-class flags and evaluate dry-running rows against SOFT/MEDIUM/HARD final-stint windows, while wet/intermediate rows get simpler continuation or switch-pressure features. Keep the first experiment compact: class-specific can-finish, option count, and class-mismatch transition flags.
expected_effect: Small AUC gain with medium confidence; likely most useful for rare wet/intermediate rows and false-pressure reduction on non-slick rows.
risk: Wet rows are sparse, so detailed wet constants may overfit. Use coarse class features first and avoid target-derived wet priors unless fold-safe.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
